generated_requirements.text: The system shall retrieve live event and activity data from both the Sync Matrix and Exercise Management Tool applications via defined integration interfaces.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core integration with upstream data sources is the foundation of the real-time display tool's purpose.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically refresh all displayed data at a configurable interval not exceeding 30 seconds to reflect the current state of ongoing exercises.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time situational awareness requires frequent, bounded data refresh to keep coordinators informed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a dual-region interface consisting of a graphical timeline view in one region and a structured data table in a second region, both visible simultaneously.
generated_requirements.rationale: The dual-region layout is the defining UI paradigm described in the use case and must be explicitly specified.

generated_requirements.text: The graphical timeline view shall display all scheduled Events and Activities plotted against a time axis, with each item represented as a labeled bar spanning its scheduled start and end times.
generated_requirements.rationale: A time-axis bar representation is the standard mechanism for communicating schedule relationships in exercise management.

generated_requirements.text: The structured data table shall display, at minimum, the following columns for each Activity: Activity ID, Activity Name, Venue, Scheduled Start Time, Scheduled End Time, Actual Start Time, Actual End Time, and Status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defining minimum required columns ensures coordinators have the essential data fields needed for exercise tracking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply color-coded visual indicators to both the timeline bars and data table rows to communicate the scheduling alignment status of each activity: green for on-schedule (within 0–5 minutes of scheduled time), yellow for minor deviation (6–15 minutes), and red for major deviation (greater than 15 minutes).
generated_requirements.rationale: Color-coded indicators provide immediate at-a-glance status without requiring coordinators to interpret raw timestamps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize the selection state between the timeline view and the data table such that selecting an item in one region highlights the corresponding item in the other region.
generated_requirements.rationale: Synchronized selection prevents cognitive mismatch between the two display regions and improves usability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a venue filter control that allows users to display data for one or more selected venues, hiding activities associated with unselected venues from both the timeline and data table.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-venue exercises require focused filtering so coordinators can isolate relevant operational areas.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a 'Key Events Only' toggle that, when activated, filters both display regions to show only activities flagged as key events, hiding all non-key activities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Key event filtering reduces information overload during high-tempo exercise phases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to customize the visible columns in the data table, including the ability to show, hide, and reorder columns.
generated_requirements.rationale: Column customization accommodates different roles that prioritize different data fields during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to sort the data table by any visible column in ascending or descending order.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sortable columns enable coordinators to quickly locate late-starting or overdue activities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a free-text search field that filters both display regions in real time to show only items whose name, ID, or venue contains the entered string.
generated_requirements.rationale: Free-text search accelerates locating specific activities in large, complex exercise schedules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually distinguish overlapping activities within the same venue on the timeline view by rendering them in separate horizontal swim lanes within that venue's row.
generated_requirements.rationale: Overlapping activities must not be rendered on top of each other, as this would obscure schedule conflicts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a visual 'current time' marker (e.g., a vertical line) on the timeline view that advances in real time to indicate the present moment relative to scheduled activities.
generated_requirements.rationale: A live time marker allows coordinators to instantly assess which activities are currently in progress or overdue.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall highlight activities that have started later than their scheduled start time with a distinct visual indicator (e.g., a hatched pattern or offset bar) on the timeline view.
generated_requirements.rationale: Late-start visualization is an explicit edge case that must be surfaced to coordinators without relying solely on color.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a deep-link mechanism that, when a user selects an activity or event, opens the corresponding record directly in the source application (Sync Matrix or Exercise Management Tool) in a new browser tab or window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deep links enable coordinators to navigate to source applications for data modifications without manual searching.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, granting access only to users assigned the roles of Exercise Coordinator or Operations Staff, and denying access to all other users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting access to authorized roles protects sensitive homeland security exercise data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate users against the organization's centralized identity provider using a standards-based protocol (e.g., SAML 2.0 or OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect) before granting access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralized authentication ensures consistent identity management and avoids siloed credential stores.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated user session after 30 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication before restoring access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout mitigates the risk of unauthorized access from unattended workstations in an operations center.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the display tool and its upstream source applications shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive exercise data from interception on shared or untrusted networks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an immutable audit log recording each user login, logout, filter change, deep-link navigation, and report generation event, including the user identity, timestamp, and action details.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging supports accountability and post-exercise review of coordinator actions during the exercise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect when the live data feed from either upstream source is interrupted and display a prominent, persistent banner notification indicating the affected source and the timestamp of the last successful data refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: Coordinators must be immediately aware of data feed failures to avoid acting on stale information.

generated_requirements.text: When a live data feed is interrupted, the system shall continue to display the most recently retrieved data and clearly mark all affected items as 'Data Stale' until the feed is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation with stale-data labeling prevents a blank display while still warning users of reduced data fidelity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically attempt to reconnect to an interrupted data feed at intervals not exceeding 60 seconds without requiring user intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic reconnection minimizes the duration of data gaps during transient network or service interruptions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate historical exercise reports that include a time-stamped record of all Events and Activities, their scheduled versus actual times, status progressions, and venue assignments for a selected exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: Historical reporting supports after-action reviews and compliance documentation for homeland security exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to export historical reports in at least two formats: PDF and CSV.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple export formats accommodate both human-readable reporting and downstream data analysis needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to filter historical reports by date range, venue, activity status, and role of the responsible coordinator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Granular report filtering enables targeted after-action analysis without requiring users to process the full dataset.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous display of activities across a minimum of 20 venues on the timeline view without degradation of rendering performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Large-scale homeland security exercises may involve many venues, and the UI must scale accordingly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render an initial page load and display populated data within 5 seconds under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A fast initial load ensures coordinators can access situational awareness quickly at the start of or during an exercise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply filter and sort operations and update both display regions within 2 seconds of user input.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive filtering is critical during high-tempo exercise phases where coordinators need immediate visual feedback.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of 99.5% or greater during scheduled exercise windows, as measured over any rolling 30-day period.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during exercise windows is essential because downtime directly impairs exercise execution and safety.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be operable on the latest two major versions of Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, and Microsoft Edge without requiring browser plugins or extensions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser compatibility ensures the tool is accessible from standard government and contractor workstations.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, including sufficient color contrast ratios and keyboard navigability for all interactive elements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance ensures the tool is usable by personnel with visual or motor impairments and meets federal requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide tooltips or hover-over detail panels on timeline bars that display the full activity name, venue, scheduled times, actual times, and current status without requiring navigation away from the timeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline detail panels reduce context switching and allow coordinators to inspect activity details without losing their place.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to zoom in and out on the timeline view's time axis (e.g., from a full-exercise view down to a 1-hour window) using mouse scroll, pinch gesture, or dedicated zoom controls.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timeline zoom enables coordinators to shift between strategic overview and tactical detail as needed during an exercise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall persist each user's filter selections, column customizations, and zoom level across sessions so that their preferred view is restored upon next login.
generated_requirements.rationale: Persisting user preferences reduces setup time at the start of each exercise session.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 50 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of refresh rate or UI responsiveness.
generated_requirements.rationale: Large exercises involve many simultaneous coordinators and operations staff who must all receive timely data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data received from upstream sources against a defined schema before rendering it, and shall log and discard any records that fail validation without crashing the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation protects the display from rendering corrupt or malformed data that could mislead coordinators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that the data displayed in the timeline view and the data table is always derived from the same data snapshot, preventing inconsistencies between the two regions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data consistency between display regions is critical to prevent coordinators from acting on contradictory information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrative interface allowing system administrators to manage user accounts, assign roles, and revoke access without requiring code changes or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Runtime user management is necessary to onboard or remove personnel quickly as exercise team compositions change.

generated_requirements.text: The system's integration interfaces with Sync Matrix and Exercise Management Tool shall be implemented using documented, versioned APIs, and the system shall log the API version in use at startup.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned API integration reduces the risk of silent breakage when upstream applications are updated.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configuration file or administrative settings panel that allows the data refresh interval, session timeout duration, and color-coding thresholds to be modified without code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalizing key operational parameters allows the system to be tuned for different exercise types without developer involvement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a distinct visual status for activities in each of the following states: Scheduled (not yet started), In Progress, Completed On Time, Completed Late, and Cancelled.
generated_requirements.rationale: Explicit lifecycle state visualization gives coordinators a complete picture of exercise execution progress.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to manually trigger an immediate data refresh on demand, independent of the automatic refresh cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: On-demand refresh allows coordinators to force an update immediately after a known change in the source system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the timestamp of the most recent successful data refresh prominently in the user interface at all times.
generated_requirements.rationale: Showing the last refresh time allows users to independently assess data currency without relying solely on system alerts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-venue scenarios by grouping timeline rows and data table entries by venue, with collapsible venue sections to allow coordinators to focus on specific operational areas.
generated_requirements.rationale: Collapsible venue groupings manage visual complexity in exercises spanning many simultaneous venues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unhandled application error and restore the last known good display state within 10 seconds, without requiring a full page reload by the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic error recovery minimizes disruption to coordinators during critical exercise execution phases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not store any exercise data locally on the client device beyond the current browser session, ensuring sensitive data is not persisted on shared workstations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preventing local data persistence protects sensitive homeland security exercise information on shared or public terminals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a new-user onboarding walkthrough or contextual help overlay that introduces the dual-region interface, filter controls, color-coding legend, and deep-link functionality on first login.
generated_requirements.rationale: An onboarding guide reduces the learning curve for new coordinators who must operate effectively under time pressure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall include a color-coding legend permanently visible in the user interface that defines the meaning of each status color and pattern used in the timeline and data table.
generated_requirements.rationale: A persistent legend ensures all users can correctly interpret visual indicators without prior memorization or training.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS listings by criteria including property type, price range, location, number of bedrooms, number of bathrooms, square footage, and listing status.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS search is the core field workflow enabling realtors to find and present relevant properties to clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display high-resolution property images in a scrollable gallery view for each MLS listing, supporting pinch-to-zoom interaction on touch-enabled devices.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual property inspection via images is essential for realtors conducting client presentations in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display an interactive map for each property listing showing its geographic location, nearby amenities, and surrounding listings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Map views provide spatial context that supports property evaluation and client decision-making.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions from the realtor's current GPS location to any selected property listing by integrating with a third-party mapping and directions service.
generated_requirements.rationale: Driving directions enable field realtors to navigate efficiently between property showings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable realtors to generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report by selecting comparable sold, active, and expired listings, and shall produce a formatted, printable or shareable report summarizing valuation data.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA generation is a primary tool for supporting property valuations and client presentations in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save, edit, and delete draft CMA reports, retaining them locally on the device until explicitly submitted or discarded.
generated_requirements.rationale: Draft persistence ensures realtors can iteratively refine CMA reports across sessions without data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to initiate a phone call to a client contact directly from within the application using the device's native calling capability.
generated_requirements.rationale: In-app call initiation reduces context switching and supports seamless client communication in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to compose and send appointment requests to clients, specifying date, time, location, and a message, and shall deliver these requests via email or SMS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment request functionality supports scheduling coordination between realtors and clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall receive and display client responses to appointment requests, updating the appointment status (accepted, declined, or proposed new time) in the realtor's calendar.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tracking appointment responses ensures realtors have accurate, up-to-date scheduling information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize the realtor's contact list with the office contact management system, reflecting additions, updates, and deletions bidirectionally within a configurable sync interval not to exceed 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Bidirectional contact synchronization ensures field realtors always have current client information consistent with office records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize the realtor's calendar with the office calendar system, reflecting all appointments, meetings, and events bidirectionally within a configurable sync interval not to exceed 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Calendar synchronization prevents scheduling conflicts between field activities and office commitments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in an offline mode, allowing realtors to access previously cached MLS listings, property images, maps, contacts, calendar entries, and CMA drafts without a network connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline capability ensures continuous productivity when network connectivity is unavailable in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect network connectivity changes and seamlessly transition between online and offline modes without requiring user intervention or application restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent mode switching prevents workflow interruption when connectivity fluctuates in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue all data changes made in offline mode and automatically synchronize them with backend systems upon restoration of network connectivity, resolving conflicts using a last-write-wins strategy with user notification of any conflicts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline change queuing with conflict resolution ensures data integrity across disconnected and reconnected sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate realtors using a username and password credential pair, enforcing a minimum password complexity policy of at least 10 characters including uppercase, lowercase, numeric, and special characters.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strong authentication restricts system access to authorized realtors and protects sensitive client and property data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multi-factor authentication (MFA), requiring realtors to verify identity via a second factor (e.g., TOTP or SMS code) upon login from an unrecognized device.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA provides an additional security layer against unauthorized access, particularly important for mobile devices that may be lost or stolen.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based authorization, ensuring that only users with a verified licensed realtor role can access MLS data, CMA tools, and client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control ensures that sensitive real estate data is accessible only to appropriately credentialed users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock the application and require re-authentication after a configurable period of inactivity, defaulting to 5 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-lock mitigates the risk of unauthorized access if a device is left unattended in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a self-service installation package that can be downloaded and installed by the realtor on a supported smart device without requiring IT assistance or formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-installing capability reduces deployment friction and supports rapid onboarding of field realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an in-app client relationship management (CRM) view allowing realtors to view, add, edit, and tag client records with property preferences, interaction history, and notes.
generated_requirements.rationale: CRM functionality enables realtors to manage client relationships and personalize service delivery from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to attach MLS listings to client records and flag them as 'interested', 'not interested', or 'under consideration' to track client preferences.
generated_requirements.rationale: Linking listings to client preferences supports targeted property recommendations and improves client service.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the MLS database via a published API, retrieving listing data in real time when online and refreshing the local cache at a minimum interval of every 30 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular MLS data refresh ensures realtors work with current listing information to avoid presenting stale data to clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS search results within 3 seconds of query submission under normal network conditions (minimum 4G/LTE connectivity).
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response times are critical to maintaining a fluid, professional experience during client-facing field interactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load a full property detail page, including images and map, within 5 seconds on a 4G/LTE connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid property detail loading prevents delays during live client presentations and property showings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and render a completed CMA report within 10 seconds of the realtor initiating report generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely CMA generation supports real-time client consultations without disruptive wait times.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 simultaneous authenticated users without degradation of response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability to thousands of concurrent users is a stated platform requirement to support enterprise-wide realtor adoption.

generated_requirements.text: The system's backend infrastructure shall scale horizontally and automatically provision additional compute resources when average CPU utilization exceeds 70% for more than 60 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-scaling ensures the platform maintains performance SLAs during peak usage periods without manual intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all data at rest on the device and backend servers using AES-256 encryption.
generated_requirements.rationale: End-to-end encryption protects sensitive client, property, and authentication data from interception and unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store authentication tokens securely using the device's native secure enclave or keystore, and shall never persist plaintext credentials on the device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure token storage prevents credential theft in the event of device compromise or unauthorized physical access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall invalidate all active sessions and wipe locally cached sensitive data upon remote wipe command issued by an administrator, completing the operation within 60 seconds of command receipt.
generated_requirements.rationale: Remote wipe capability protects sensitive data if a realtor's device is lost or stolen.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a realtor account for 30 minutes after 5 consecutive failed login attempts and notify the account administrator via email.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies mitigate brute-force credential attacks on the mobile application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log of all authentication events, data access actions, and synchronization operations, retaining logs for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging supports compliance, forensic investigation, and accountability for access to sensitive real estate and client data.

generated_requirements.text: A new realtor with no prior experience using the application shall be able to complete a full property search, view a listing detail, and send an appointment request within 10 minutes of first launch, without consulting any documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: The no-training mandate requires that core workflows be discoverable and completable by first-time users within a measurable time threshold.

generated_requirements.text: The application's user interface shall conform to platform-native design guidelines (Apple Human Interface Guidelines for iOS, Material Design for Android) to leverage familiar interaction patterns.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adherence to native design conventions reduces the learning curve and supports the no-formal-training requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual in-app guidance, including tooltips and inline help text, for all non-obvious UI elements and workflows such as CMA configuration and conflict resolution.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contextual help supports self-directed learning and reduces errors without requiring formal training sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 80 or above in usability testing conducted with a representative sample of licensed realtors.
generated_requirements.rationale: A quantified SUS target provides a measurable acceptance criterion for the intuitive-use mandate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum uptime of 99.9% (measured monthly), excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability ensures realtors can rely on the platform during business-critical field activities without unexpected outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned service outage and restore full functionality within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 1 hour and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RTO and RPO targets ensure business continuity and limit data loss in the event of a system failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully functional on iOS (version 15 and above) and Android (version 10 and above) operating systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-platform support on current major mobile OS versions ensures broad device coverage across the realtor user base.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver a consistent feature set and user experience across all supported platforms, with platform-specific adaptations limited to native UI conventions and hardware capabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Feature parity across platforms prevents a fragmented user experience and reduces support complexity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support integration with at least two major MLS data providers via standardized RESO Web API interfaces, with the ability to add new MLS integrations through configuration rather than code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configuration-driven MLS integration reduces the cost and time required to onboard new data providers as the platform scales.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a documented integration API for office calendar and contact systems, supporting at minimum Microsoft Exchange, Google Workspace, and iCloud, using OAuth 2.0 for authorization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standardized integration with common office platforms ensures broad compatibility with the diverse back-office systems used by real estate agencies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor with an in-app alert and push notification within 60 seconds of receiving a client response to an appointment request.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely appointment response notifications enable realtors to react quickly to scheduling changes while in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to share a CMA report with a client via email or a secure shareable link directly from the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: In-app report sharing streamlines the delivery of valuation materials to clients without requiring separate tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support biometric authentication (Face ID, Touch ID, or equivalent Android biometric API) as an alternative to password entry for subsequent logins after initial credential-based authentication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Biometric login improves usability and speed of access while maintaining a strong authentication posture for field realtors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall consume no more than 10% of device battery per hour of active use under typical field usage patterns (search, map viewing, and communication).
generated_requirements.rationale: Battery efficiency is critical for field realtors who may be away from charging sources for extended periods during the workday.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall maintain a minimum test coverage of 80% for unit and integration tests, and all public APIs shall have corresponding contract tests.
generated_requirements.rationale: High test coverage reduces regression risk and supports confident, rapid iteration on a platform serving thousands of users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all integration errors with external systems (MLS, calendar, contacts, mapping) and surface actionable error messages to the realtor within the application when an integration failure affects their current workflow.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent error handling for integration failures prevents silent data inconsistencies and helps realtors understand and work around disruptions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, edit, and delete cohorts of nursing students, including assigning a cohort name, start quarter, and associated Program of Study.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cohort management is the foundational administrative workflow described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to add, remove, and transfer individual students between cohorts, recording the effective date of each change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Student membership changes are a core lifecycle event that must be tracked for administrative accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to define and maintain Programs of Study, including specifying required classroom and clinical courses, their sequence, and the quarters in which they are offered.
generated_requirements.rationale: Programs of Study drive scheduling logic and student progress tracking throughout the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support scheduling of both classroom courses and clinical courses across multiple quarters, allowing Program Administrators to assign courses to specific quarters within a cohort's Program of Study.
generated_requirements.rationale: Quarter-based scheduling is a primary operational function described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track each student's individual progress through their Program of Study, recording completed, in-progress, and remaining courses per quarter.
generated_requirements.rationale: Individual progress tracking is explicitly required to support administrative planning and student oversight.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members to create, edit, and delete clinical site assignments, associating a student or group of students with a specific clinical site, supervisor, and scheduled dates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clinical site assignment management is identified as operationally complex and central to the platform's purpose.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable capacity limits per clinical site per scheduling period, preventing assignments that exceed the defined maximum number of students.
generated_requirements.rationale: Capacity constraints at clinical sites are a key scheduling rule that must be enforced to avoid over-assignment errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, edit, and delete lab sections, including specifying the section name, associated course, instructor, room, time, and maximum enrollment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Lab section management is explicitly listed as a core administrative function of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Nursing Staff Members to assign students to lab sections and enforce enrollment limits, preventing over-enrollment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enrollment control in lab sections prevents scheduling conflicts and resource overuse.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to affected students, instructors, and relevant staff members whenever a schedule change, clinical section modification, or cohort arrangement change occurs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated notifications are explicitly required to keep all stakeholders informed of changes in real time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define and enforce configurable notification trigger rules, specifying which events (e.g., cohort transfer, clinical site reassignment, lab section change) generate notifications and to which recipient roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: Precise notification triggers and recipient rules are called out in the elicitation strategy as a key requirement area.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate printable reports for cohorts, individual students, clinical sites, and lab sections, with each report type including configurable fields and exportable in at least PDF format.
generated_requirements.rationale: Printable report generation is explicitly listed as a required output to support administrative and regulatory planning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a cohort report that lists all students in a cohort, their current course enrollments, clinical site assignments, and overall program progress.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cohort-level reporting is needed for administrators to assess group-level scheduling and progress at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a student report that details an individual student's program of study, completed and remaining courses, lab section assignments, and clinical site history.
generated_requirements.rationale: Individual student reports support advising, compliance verification, and administrative planning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a clinical site report that lists all students and instructors assigned to a site for a given scheduling period, along with site capacity and utilization metrics.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clinical site reports are required to support site coordination and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a lab section report that lists enrolled students, the assigned instructor, room, schedule, and current enrollment versus capacity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Lab section reports are needed for instructors and administrators to manage section logistics.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with DePaul University's CampusConnect system to import and synchronize student enrollment records, course catalog data, and academic calendar information on a scheduled basis no less than once per day.
generated_requirements.rationale: CampusConnect integration is explicitly required to keep the platform aligned with authoritative university data sources.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with DePaul University's faculty and student servers to authenticate users and retrieve faculty and student profile information, using the university's existing identity provider.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration with university servers for authentication avoids duplicate credential management and leverages existing infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and log synchronization failures with CampusConnect or university servers, alert the Program Administrator via email within 15 minutes of a failure, and queue pending updates for retry upon restoration of connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Failure-handling expectations for integrations are called out in the elicitation strategy as a required concern.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at minimum two distinct roles: Program Administrator and Nursing Staff Member, each with a defined set of permitted actions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control is explicitly required to protect sensitive student and clinical site information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to sensitive student records (e.g., grades, health information, clinical evaluations) to authorized roles only, denying access and logging the attempt for any unauthorized access request.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protection of sensitive student data is a stated security requirement and likely subject to FERPA compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, tamper-evident audit log of all create, read, update, and delete operations performed on cohorts, student records, clinical site assignments, lab sections, and schedules, recording the acting user, timestamp, and before/after values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auditability is listed as a key quality attribute and is essential for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to search and filter audit logs by user, date range, record type, and action type, and export the results in CSV or PDF format.
generated_requirements.rationale: Searchable audit logs are necessary for administrators to investigate incidents and produce compliance evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all scheduling inputs to detect and prevent conflicts such as a student being assigned to two clinical sites at the same time, or a lab section scheduled in a room already in use.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict detection is critical to the reliability of the scheduling function, which is identified as error-prone.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to define and manage quarter calendars, including start dates, end dates, and add/drop deadlines, which govern scheduling constraints across the platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Quarter calendar management underpins all scheduling logic and must be configurable to reflect the university academic calendar.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard view for Program Administrators displaying key metrics such as total active cohorts, students per cohort, clinical site utilization, and upcoming schedule changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: A dashboard supports day-to-day administrative oversight and quick identification of scheduling issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Nursing Staff Members to view (but not modify) cohort compositions, student progress records, and clinical site assignments within their assigned scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: Read-only access for Nursing Staff Members supports operational awareness while enforcing the principle of least privilege.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support bulk import of student data via a structured file format (e.g., CSV or Excel), with validation feedback identifying any records that fail to import and the reason for failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Bulk import reduces manual data entry burden and supports initial population of cohorts at the start of each academic year.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to configure which user roles receive which categories of automated email notifications, and to customize notification email templates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable notification rules and templates ensure communications meet departmental and regulatory standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain historical records of all past cohort compositions, schedules, clinical site assignments, and lab section enrollments for a minimum of seven years to support regulatory audits.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term data retention is required for regulatory compliance in nursing education programs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any user-initiated page load or data retrieval action within 3 seconds under normal operating load (up to 100 concurrent users).
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive performance is essential for day-to-day usability by administrators and staff managing time-sensitive schedules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the generation of any printable report within 10 seconds for datasets up to 500 student records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Report generation speed directly impacts administrative efficiency during high-demand periods such as quarter start.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all Program Administrator accounts.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA reduces the risk of unauthorized access to sensitive student and clinical data by privileged accounts.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the client and server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive student and clinical site information from interception.

generated_requirements.text: All sensitive data stored in the database (including student PII and clinical site details) shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or equivalent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest ensures data remains protected in the event of unauthorized physical or logical database access.

generated_requirements.text: User sessions shall automatically expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, requiring re-authentication before the session can resume.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout limits exposure from unattended workstations in shared administrative environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards to ensure usability for staff members with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance is both a legal obligation and a usability requirement for a university administrative platform.

generated_requirements.text: A new Program Administrator with no prior system training shall be able to complete core tasks (create a cohort, assign students, generate a report) within 30 minutes of first use, supported by in-application guidance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of use is explicitly stated as a design goal for day-to-day nursing program administration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help text and tooltips for all complex scheduling and clinical assignment fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline guidance reduces errors in operationally complex workflows such as clinical site assignment and quarter scheduling.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an uptime of at least 99.5% measured monthly, excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 48 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is required to support continuous administrative operations across academic quarters.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned outage and restore full service within 1 hour (RTO), with a maximum data loss of 15 minutes (RPO), supported by automated backups.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RTO and RPO targets ensure the platform can recover quickly from failures without significant data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated database backups at least every 15 minutes and store backups in a geographically separate location from the primary server.
generated_requirements.rationale: Frequent off-site backups protect against data loss from hardware failure or disaster events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be built using a modular, layered architecture with documented APIs for each integration point, enabling individual components (e.g., notification engine, report generator) to be updated or replaced independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future changes to individual subsystems.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase shall maintain a minimum of 80% unit test coverage for all business logic modules, with automated tests executed on every code commit via a CI/CD pipeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated test coverage ensures that changes to scheduling and notification logic do not introduce regressions.

generated_requirements.text: All integration interfaces with CampusConnect and university servers shall use documented, versioned APIs, and the system shall support at least one prior API version concurrently to allow for phased upgrades.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned APIs prevent integration breakage when upstream university systems are updated independently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on university-managed infrastructure (on-premises or private cloud) and shall not depend on any third-party SaaS services that are not approved by DePaul University IT.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deployment flexibility on university-controlled infrastructure ensures compliance with institutional IT governance policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the latest two major versions of all major modern web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) without requiring browser plugins or extensions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Broad browser compatibility ensures accessibility for all staff regardless of their workstation configuration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all outbound notification emails, including recipient, timestamp, triggering event, and delivery status (sent, failed, bounced), and make these logs accessible to Program Administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Notification delivery logs are essential for verifying that required communications reached their intended recipients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly error message and suggested corrective action whenever a scheduling conflict, capacity violation, or data validation failure is detected, without exposing internal system details.
generated_requirements.rationale: Actionable error messages reduce user frustration and help staff resolve scheduling issues quickly and correctly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable scheduled-maintenance mode that disables write operations and displays an informational banner to users, while still allowing read-only access to schedules and reports.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful maintenance mode minimizes disruption to staff who need to view schedules even during system updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support three distinct user roles: Cardmember Services Representative (CSR), Merchant Services Representative (MSR), and System Administrator, each with a uniquely defined set of permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role differentiation is the foundation of the access control model described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict CSRs to viewing, creating, and modifying dispute cases only for cardholders within their authorized cardholder base, preventing access to any cardholder account outside that scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scoped access ensures CSRs cannot view or alter records belonging to cardholders they are not authorized to service.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict MSRs to viewing and responding to dispute cases only for merchants within their authorized merchant portfolio, preventing access to merchant accounts outside that scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scoped access for MSRs mirrors the cardholder-base restriction applied to CSRs, protecting merchant data confidentiality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall grant System Administrators the ability to manage user accounts, assign and revoke role-based permissions, configure system parameters, and view all dispute cases across all cardholder and merchant populations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Administrators require elevated, cross-portfolio access to maintain the system and its user base.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via a secure login mechanism before granting access to any application function, and shall enforce session timeouts after a configurable period of inactivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication and session management are baseline security controls for a web-based financial application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all System Administrator accounts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Elevated privileges of administrator accounts warrant stronger authentication to reduce the risk of unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to search for existing dispute cases using at least the following criteria: cardholder account number, merchant ID, transaction date range, dispute status, case ID, and dispute type.
generated_requirements.rationale: Flexible search capability is essential for representatives to efficiently locate cases during day-to-day operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow CSRs to initiate a new dispute case by capturing at minimum: cardholder account number, disputed transaction reference, transaction amount, transaction date, dispute reason code, and a free-text description of the dispute.
generated_requirements.rationale: These fields constitute the minimum data set required to open a valid dispute case and satisfy regulatory documentation requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the creation of Ticket Retrieval Requests (TRRs) against a disputed transaction, capturing the retrieval reason, requested documentation type, and target merchant, and shall transmit the request to the merchant via the integrated back-end systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: TRRs are a standard pre-chargeback step that allows the issuer to obtain transaction documentation from the merchant.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the creation of Chargeback Notifications against a disputed transaction, capturing the chargeback reason code, disputed amount, and supporting documentation references, and shall initiate the chargeback process through the integrated billing system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Chargeback processing is a core function of the dispute lifecycle and must be formally tracked and transmitted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall manage dispute cases through a defined lifecycle with the following states: Opened, Pending Documentation, Under Review, Chargeback Initiated, Resolved – Cardholder Favor, Resolved – Merchant Favor, and Closed, enforcing valid state transitions and recording the timestamp and acting user for each transition.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-defined state machine ensures consistent case handling and provides a clear audit trail of case progression.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically escalate a dispute case to 'Chargeback Initiated' state if a Ticket Retrieval Request remains unanswered by the merchant beyond the configurable response deadline, and shall notify the responsible CSR.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated escalation enforces regulatory and network timelines without requiring manual monitoring of every open TRR.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Regulation E timelines by preventing the initiation of a dispute on an electronic funds transfer transaction more than 60 days after the cardholder's statement date on which the error first appeared, and shall display a compliance warning when a case approaches this deadline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulation E mandates a 60-day window for cardholders to report EFT errors, and the system must enforce this to maintain regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Regulation Z timelines by ensuring that billing error disputes on credit card transactions are initiated within 60 days of the statement date on which the alleged error appeared, and shall block case creation beyond this window with an explanatory message.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulation Z requires issuers to acknowledge billing error notices within specific timeframes, making deadline enforcement a compliance obligation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Merchant Operating Regulation chargeback timeframes by preventing submission of a chargeback notification beyond the network-mandated deadline (configurable per card network) and alerting the CSR when a case is within a configurable warning threshold of that deadline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Card network operating regulations impose strict chargeback filing windows; missing them forfeits the issuer's right to recover funds.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically generate and queue a provisional credit to the cardholder's account through the billing system integration within one business day of a dispute case being opened for transactions subject to Regulation E, and shall record the credit reference in the case record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulation E requires issuers to provisionally credit disputed EFT amounts while the investigation is pending.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically reverse a provisional credit through the billing system if a dispute investigation is resolved in the merchant's favor, and shall generate a corresponding notification letter to the cardholder.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provisional credit reversal upon merchant-favorable resolution is required by Regulation E and must be accompanied by cardholder notification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the cardmember database to retrieve and display cardholder account details (name, account number, billing address, account status, and transaction history) when a dispute case is created or viewed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time account data retrieval ensures representatives work with accurate, current cardholder information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the merchant database to retrieve and display merchant details (merchant name, merchant ID, category code, contact information, and account status) when a TRR or chargeback notification is created or viewed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate merchant data is required to correctly route retrieval requests and chargebacks to the appropriate merchant.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the billing system to submit financial adjustments (provisional credits, credit reversals, and final settlements) as atomic transactions, ensuring that a billing adjustment is either fully committed or fully rolled back with an error recorded on the case.
generated_requirements.rationale: Atomic billing transactions prevent partial financial adjustments that could result in incorrect account balances.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the letter printing utility to generate, format, and queue correspondence to merchants and cardholders at defined case lifecycle events (e.g., TRR issuance, chargeback notification, resolution notice), capturing the letter reference number and generation timestamp in the case record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated correspondence generation ensures timely, consistent communication required by regulation and operating rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle integration failures with back-end systems (cardmember database, merchant database, billing system, letter printing utility) gracefully by displaying a user-facing error message, logging the failure with full context, and, where applicable, queuing the failed operation for automatic retry up to a configurable number of attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Robust error handling prevents silent data loss and ensures failed operations are surfaced and retried rather than dropped.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit trail for every dispute case, recording each user action and system-generated event with the actor identity (user ID or system process name), action type, affected data fields (before and after values), and UTC timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive audit trail is required for regulatory examination, fraud investigation, and dispute resolution accountability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent modification or deletion of any audit trail record by any user role, including System Administrators, and shall detect and alert on any unauthorized attempt to alter audit data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Immutability of audit records is a regulatory and forensic requirement; tampering must be technically prevented, not merely prohibited by policy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to add follow-up notes, attach supporting documents (PDF, JPEG, PNG, up to 10 MB per file), and log follow-up actions to an open dispute case, with each entry attributed to the submitting user and timestamped.
generated_requirements.rationale: Follow-up documentation capability supports the ongoing investigation process and provides evidence for regulatory review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard view for each user role displaying their assigned open cases, cases approaching regulatory or network deadlines within a configurable threshold, and cases awaiting their action.
generated_requirements.rationale: A role-specific dashboard enables representatives to prioritize work and avoid missing time-sensitive regulatory obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated in-application and email notifications to the responsible representative when a case deadline is within a configurable warning period, when a case state changes, or when a back-end system action (e.g., billing adjustment) fails.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive notifications reduce the risk of missed deadlines and keep representatives informed of case changes without requiring constant manual monitoring.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow System Administrators to configure business rules including: regulatory deadline windows, chargeback network deadlines per card network, provisional credit timing thresholds, retry attempt limits for back-end integrations, and notification warning thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable business rules allow the system to adapt to regulatory changes and network rule updates without requiring code deployments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce management reports including: case volume by dispute type and reason code, average case resolution time, chargeback acceptance and reversal rates, cases resolved within regulatory deadlines vs. out-of-compliance, and representative productivity metrics.
generated_requirements.rationale: Management reporting supports operational oversight, regulatory self-assessment, and continuous process improvement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return search results for dispute case queries within 3 seconds for result sets of up to 500 records under normal operating load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response is critical for representative productivity in a high-volume case management environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 300 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of response times beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: The application serves multiple internal and external representative populations simultaneously, requiring adequate concurrency capacity.

generated_requirements.text: All pages and forms within the application shall load and render within 4 seconds under peak load conditions as measured at the server side.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent page load performance ensures representatives can work efficiently without system-induced delays.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% (measured monthly), excluding pre-approved maintenance windows, given its role in time-sensitive regulatory dispute processing.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is essential because missed regulatory deadlines due to system downtime can result in financial and legal liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated failover for all critical back-end integration points so that a single integration endpoint failure does not render the entire application unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Partial integration failures should be isolated to prevent cascading outages that affect unrelated case management functions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher and all sensitive data at rest (including cardholder account numbers, transaction data, and audit logs) using AES-256 encryption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of financial and personal data in transit and at rest is required by PCI DSS and applicable data protection regulations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall mask cardholder account numbers in all UI displays, logs, and exported reports, showing only the last four digits, except where full display is explicitly required for a specific authorized workflow.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account number masking reduces the risk of inadvertent exposure of sensitive cardholder data in compliance with PCI DSS requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed authentication attempts and automatically lock a user account after a configurable number of consecutive failed login attempts, notifying the System Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies protect against brute-force credential attacks on a system containing sensitive financial data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all user-supplied input on both the client and server sides to prevent injection attacks (SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF), rejecting and logging any request containing malicious payloads.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation is a fundamental defense against the most common web application attack vectors in a financial system.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards to ensure usability by representatives with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance ensures the application is usable by all internal and external representatives regardless of disability status.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide inline contextual help and field-level validation messages that clearly explain data entry requirements and error conditions without requiring the user to consult external documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contextual guidance reduces training time and data entry errors in a complex, regulation-driven workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed using a modular, service-oriented architecture so that individual integration adapters (cardmember database, merchant database, billing system, letter printing utility) can be updated or replaced independently without requiring changes to core case management logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular integration design reduces the cost and risk of adapting to back-end system changes or replacements over time.

generated_requirements.text: All business rule configurations (deadline windows, reason code mappings, notification thresholds) shall be stored in an externalized configuration store and shall not require application redeployment to take effect.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalized configuration enables rapid response to regulatory changes without the risk and delay of a full software release cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on standard enterprise web application infrastructure (Linux-based OS, containerized deployment) and shall not depend on proprietary hardware or operating system features.
generated_requirements.rationale: Platform independence reduces infrastructure lock-in and simplifies migration to alternative hosting environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support horizontal scaling of application server instances to handle increased load without architectural changes, with load balancing distributing requests across all active instances.
generated_requirements.rationale: Horizontal scalability ensures the system can accommodate growth in case volume and user population without redesign.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain referential integrity between dispute cases, associated TRRs, chargeback notifications, billing adjustments, and audit trail entries, ensuring that no case component can exist without a valid parent case record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Referential integrity prevents orphaned records that could cause data inconsistencies and complicate regulatory audits.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform and log a reconciliation check between billing system adjustment records and dispute case financial records on a daily basis, flagging any discrepancies for System Administrator review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Daily reconciliation detects financial discrepancies early, protecting against undetected errors in provisional credits or chargeback amounts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all dispute case records, audit trail entries, and associated documents for a minimum of seven years from case closure, in compliance with applicable financial record retention regulations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term record retention is mandated by financial regulations and is necessary to support regulatory examinations and legal proceedings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a full-text search capability over case notes and attached document metadata to allow representatives and administrators to locate specific information within the case history.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full-text search over case history accelerates investigation and reduces the time required to locate relevant evidence within complex cases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Regulation E acknowledgment letter to the cardholder within 5 business days of dispute case creation for EFT disputes, using the letter printing utility integration, and shall record the letter reference and send date in the case record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulation E requires issuers to acknowledge receipt of an error notice within 10 business days; the 5-day internal target provides a compliance buffer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a complete resolution notice to the cardholder within the Regulation E-mandated 45-business-day investigation window for EFT disputes, automatically flagging cases that are at risk of breaching this deadline and escalating to a supervisor queue.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulation E requires completion of EFT error investigations within 45 business days (or 90 days for certain transactions), and automated escalation prevents regulatory violations.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application must maintain a detailed history of every action that a user takes on a dispute case. This ensures a complete audit trail if questions arise later on with regard to a particular dispute case.

ground_truth_requirements.text: All actions that modify an existing dispute case must be recorded in the case history.
