ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must be accessible by both internal and external users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent users from accessing any dispute cases that do not belong to their cardholder base.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide different levels of access with regard to disputes case initiation and follow-up actions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide view access capability for authorized users of the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide update access capability for authorized users of the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the users to select disputable transactions (based on the age of the transaction) from a user interface and initiate a dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) on the selected transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the user the ability to initiate a single dispute case on multiple transactions that belong to a single merchant.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the user to create three unique types of ticket retrieval requests. The three types of ticket retrieval requests are (1) Request for original receipt (2) Request for a copy of the receipt or (3) Request for a portfolio. A portfolio consists of documentation that would provide proof of a purchase such as the documentation that is received from a car rental agency that is more than a sales receipt.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent external users from requesting original receipts. Requests for original receipts are restricted to internal users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of ticket retrieval request that contains the following information; the dispute case number the type of retrieval requested (copy original or portfolio) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall allow the user to create or initiate a chargeback request. The system must prompt the user to enter the required information to initiate a chargeback request. The chargeback request results in a printed chargeback notification that is sent to merchant indicating that their account will be charged back if no proof is provided that the disputed transaction is valid.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a chargeback request. The confirmation must contain the following information; the dispute case number the type of chargeback requested (pending or immediate) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall prevent the creation of duplicate dispute requests 100% of the time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall accurately provide a list of possible reason codes for a given transaction. The list of possible reason codes must be derived based on the type of transaction and the industry that the merchant belongs to according to the business rules defined for the Disputes System.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the ability to view existing dispute cases.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide Search functionality. The search method must include the ability to search by (1) the dispute case number (2) the merchant account number (3) the cardmember account number and (4) the issuer number. In addition to the above criteria the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range the type of dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) the case status (open closed or all) and the dispute reason code.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color -coding the line item in the list. For example all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via follow-up actions. The list of available follow up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For any systematic (non-user initiated) action that occurs on a case such as the disputes aging process a case activity will be generated. Upon the next logon the user that initiated the dispute case must be notified that a systematic action has occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any disputes cases that have been closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a role-based access control (RBAC) mechanism that restricts each user to only the functions and data permitted for their assigned role (collision estimator, adjuster, or supervisory adjuster).
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforcing role boundaries prevents unauthorized access to sensitive claims data and privileged operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to search the recycled parts inventory by part type, part number, vehicle make, model, and year.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimators need flexible search criteria to quickly locate applicable recycled parts for a given vehicle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to filter recycled parts search results by preferred supplier.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preferred supplier filtering ensures estimators apply parts from approved vendors, supporting cost and quality controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to add one or more recycled parts from search results directly to an open collision estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless addition of recycled parts to estimates is the core workflow for estimators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to create, edit, save, and submit collision repair estimates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full estimate lifecycle management is required for estimators to complete their primary job function.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent collision estimators from accessing adjuster functions, including facility assignment, estimate review approval, and audit report generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict role separation ensures estimators cannot perform adjuster-level actions, maintaining workflow integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to assign a preferred repair facility to a claim before an estimate is created.
generated_requirements.rationale: Facility assignment by adjusters is a prerequisite step that directs where repair work and estimation will occur.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to review completed collision estimates submitted by estimators and record an approval or rejection decision with mandatory comments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adjuster review and decision-recording is central to the claims approval workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to request the generation of an audit report for a specified claim or set of claims.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit report generation is an adjuster responsibility for evaluating recycled parts usage effectiveness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate audit reports that include, at minimum: total parts used, number and percentage of recycled parts used, cost savings from recycled parts versus new parts, and the assigned repair facility.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defining the minimum audit report content ensures consistent and meaningful evaluation of recycled parts usage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to rate repair facilities on a defined scale (e.g., 1–5 stars) based on audit report outcomes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Facility ratings by supervisory adjusters provide data to guide future facility assignments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store all facility ratings with a timestamp, the identity of the supervisory adjuster who submitted the rating, and the associated audit report reference.
generated_requirements.rationale: Traceability of ratings supports accountability and enables trend analysis over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the aggregated facility rating history to adjusters when they are selecting a repair facility for a claim.
generated_requirements.rationale: Surfacing historical ratings at the point of assignment helps adjusters make informed facility selections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent adjusters from rating repair facilities; only supervisory adjusters shall have this permission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting rating authority to supervisory adjusters maintains the integrity of the facility evaluation process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the ChoiceParts external data feed to retrieve recycled parts inventory data.
generated_requirements.rationale: ChoiceParts is the designated external source for recycled parts inventory and must be integrated to support estimator workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronise recycled parts inventory data from the ChoiceParts feed at a minimum frequency of every 60 minutes during business hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular synchronisation ensures estimators work with current inventory data, reducing the risk of selecting unavailable parts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and log failures in the ChoiceParts data feed connection, and display a clear notification to estimators indicating that inventory data may be stale, including the timestamp of the last successful synchronisation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent failure notification prevents estimators from unknowingly using outdated inventory data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retry a failed ChoiceParts feed synchronisation up to five times with exponential back-off before raising a system alert to an administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated retry logic minimises the impact of transient network or feed outages on data freshness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall cache the most recently successful ChoiceParts inventory dataset so that estimators can continue searching parts during a feed outage, with a visible staleness warning.
generated_requirements.rationale: Caching maintains operational continuity during feed disruptions while keeping users informed of data currency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce strict data isolation between insurance companies (tenants) such that no user of one tenant can view, modify, or derive information from the claims data of another tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-tenant data isolation is a fundamental requirement to protect confidential claims data across competing insurers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall associate every claim, estimate, audit report, facility assignment, and facility rating with a unique tenant identifier, and all data queries shall be scoped to the authenticated user's tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tenant-scoped queries at the data layer enforce isolation and prevent cross-tenant data leakage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store tenant data in logically or physically separated data partitions, ensuring that a misconfiguration or application error cannot expose one tenant's data to another.
generated_requirements.rationale: Partition-level isolation provides a defence-in-depth layer beyond application-level tenant scoping.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via a secure login mechanism supporting at minimum username/password with enforced password complexity rules, and shall support multi-factor authentication (MFA).
generated_requirements.rationale: Strong authentication reduces the risk of unauthorised access to sensitive insurance claims data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated session after 15 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout limits exposure from unattended workstations in office and repair shop environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive claims and parts data from interception.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all sensitive data at rest, including personally identifiable information (PII) and financial claim data, using AES-256 or equivalent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects stored claims data in the event of unauthorised physical or logical storage access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log of all user actions, including logins, estimate creation and modification, part additions, facility assignments, estimate approvals/rejections, audit report requests, and facility ratings.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive audit log supports accountability, forensic investigation, and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain audit logs for a minimum of seven years in accordance with insurance industry record-keeping requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term log retention satisfies regulatory obligations and supports historical claims review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return recycled parts search results within 3 seconds for queries returning up to 200 results under normal operating load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response times keep estimators productive and reduce claim processing cycle times.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and display an audit report within 10 seconds of an adjuster's request for reports covering up to 500 claims.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely report generation supports adjuster decision-making without introducing workflow bottlenecks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 500 concurrent users across all tenants without degradation of response times beyond defined SLA thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-tenant deployment serving multiple insurance companies requires sufficient concurrency capacity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.5% measured on a monthly basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is critical for claims processing operations that span office and repair shop environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a web-based user interface accessible from standard desktop browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari — latest two major versions) without requiring additional plugin installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser-based access without plugins supports deployment in both office and repair shop environments with minimal IT overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a responsive user interface that is usable on tablet devices with screen sizes of 10 inches or larger, to support use in repair shop environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Repair shop estimators may use tablets rather than desktop workstations, requiring a responsive layout.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present recycled parts search results in a clear tabular format showing part name, part number, supplier, condition grade, price, and availability status.
generated_requirements.rationale: A well-structured results display enables estimators to quickly evaluate and select appropriate parts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide inline contextual help and tooltips for all major workflow steps, accessible without leaving the current screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contextual help reduces training burden and supports users in both office and repair shop settings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a tenant administrator to configure and manage the list of preferred suppliers visible to estimators within their tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tenant-specific supplier preferences must be configurable to reflect each insurance company's vendor agreements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a tenant administrator to create, deactivate, and assign roles to user accounts within their tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tenant-level user management enables each insurance company to independently govern its own workforce access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a tenant administrator to manage the list of approved repair facilities available for assignment within their tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Each insurer maintains its own network of approved repair facilities, requiring tenant-scoped facility management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to export audit reports in PDF and CSV formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable formats support downstream reporting, archiving, and sharing with stakeholders outside the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and display a complete version history for each estimate, showing what was changed, by whom, and when.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimate version history supports auditability and dispute resolution in the claims process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the assigned adjuster via in-application notification when an estimator submits a completed estimate for review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications prevent estimates from sitting unreviewed and keep the claims workflow moving.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the originating estimator via in-application notification when an adjuster approves or rejects their submitted estimate, including the adjuster's comments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Feedback notifications close the communication loop and allow estimators to act on rejections promptly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall calculate and display a recycled parts utilisation score for each repair facility, derived from the ratio of recycled parts used to total parts used across all audited claims for that facility within a configurable date range.
generated_requirements.rationale: A quantitative utilisation score gives supervisory adjusters an objective basis for facility ratings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to view a dashboard summarising facility ratings, recycled parts utilisation scores, and audit report counts across all facilities within their tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: A supervisory dashboard provides the high-level visibility needed to manage facility performance across the portfolio.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular architecture that allows individual components (e.g., ChoiceParts integration, reporting engine, notification service) to be updated or replaced without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular design reduces the cost and risk of future changes, particularly for the external integration layer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a health-check API endpoint that reports the status of the ChoiceParts feed connection, database connectivity, and authentication service, consumable by infrastructure monitoring tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: A health-check endpoint enables proactive monitoring and rapid diagnosis of integration or infrastructure failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned outage and restore full service within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RTO and RPO targets ensure business continuity for insurance claims operations after a failure event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate that a recycled part selected from the ChoiceParts feed is still marked as available at the time it is added to an estimate, and shall warn the estimator if the part's availability status has changed since the last synchronisation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Point-of-use availability validation reduces the risk of estimates referencing parts that are no longer obtainable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable data privacy regulations (e.g., CCPA, HIPAA where applicable) by providing mechanisms for data subject access requests and data deletion requests scoped to the relevant tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory compliance for personal data handling is mandatory for a multi-tenant insurance platform processing PII.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to search for available conference rooms by date, time range, building, floor, capacity, and available equipment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core room discovery workflow for employees must support multi-criteria filtering to efficiently locate suitable rooms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to reserve an available conference room for a specified date, time range, and set of attendees.
generated_requirements.rationale: Room reservation is the primary transactional function of the system for all employee-level users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent double-booking of a conference room by enforcing exclusive reservation locks and rejecting conflicting reservation requests in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict prevention is a critical business rule to ensure room availability integrity across concurrent users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to create a meeting agenda, attach documents, and associate the agenda with a specific room reservation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agenda management is a distinct organizer workflow that enriches meeting context beyond simple room booking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to invite internal employees and, optionally, external attendees by email address to a scheduled meeting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Invitation management is a core organizer responsibility and must support both internal and external participants.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send meeting invitations to all invited attendees via the integrated email server immediately upon meeting creation or update.
generated_requirements.rationale: Email-based invitation delivery is the primary notification channel and must be triggered automatically on relevant events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow attendees to accept, tentatively accept, or decline meeting invitations from within the application or directly via email response.
generated_requirements.rationale: RSVP functionality is required so organizers can track attendance and plan accordingly.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send configurable reminder notifications to meeting organizers and accepted attendees at user-defined intervals before the meeting start time (e.g., 24 hours, 1 hour).
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated reminders reduce no-shows and ensure participants are prepared for scheduled meetings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the meeting organizer and all attendees immediately when a reservation is cancelled, modified, or when a room becomes unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely change notifications prevent wasted travel and allow attendees to adjust their schedules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display an interactive building map for each office location, visually indicating room locations, current availability status, and room details on selection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visual building maps improve wayfinding and room selection, especially in large or unfamiliar office buildings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically update room schedule displays and availability status in real time as reservations are created, modified, or cancelled.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time schedule updates prevent stale data from causing booking conflicts or user confusion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees and organizers to reserve specific equipment items (e.g., projectors, video conferencing units, whiteboards) in association with a room reservation or independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Equipment reservation is a distinct tracked resource that must be managed alongside room bookings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track the current location, reservation status, and transport history of each equipment item across all buildings and floors.
generated_requirements.rationale: Equipment tracking ensures accountability and prevents loss or scheduling conflicts for shared assets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users to submit equipment transport requests, specifying the equipment item, origin location, destination room, and required delivery time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transport request management is required to coordinate physical movement of equipment between rooms or buildings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable cancellation policy, including minimum notice periods, and notify relevant parties when a cancellation violates policy thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cancellation policies are a key business rule that must be configurable per organization and enforced consistently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support three distinct user roles — Employee, Meeting Organizer, and Manager — each with a defined set of permissions and accessible features.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control is required to enforce appropriate privilege separation across the actor types described.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers to generate reservation reports filtered by date range, building, room, organizer, department, or equipment, and export results in at least CSV and PDF formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reporting is an elevated manager capability needed for resource utilization analysis and operational oversight.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with multiple calendar views of meeting schedules, including daily, weekly, monthly, and room-centric views, filterable by building or department.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-view schedule visibility gives managers the situational awareness needed to manage resources across the organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a hierarchical organizational structure for locations, comprising at minimum: Organization → Building → Floor → Room.
generated_requirements.rationale: A location hierarchy is necessary to manage rooms across multiple global office buildings within a single system instance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to configure room attributes including name, capacity, location, available equipment, accessibility features, and booking rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Flexible room configuration enables the system to accurately represent diverse real-world room inventories.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with at least one standard SMTP-compatible email server to send and receive meeting invitations, notifications, and RSVP responses.
generated_requirements.rationale: Email server integration is the primary external communication channel and must conform to standard protocols for broad compatibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support import and export of meeting data using standard calendar interchange formats, including iCalendar (RFC 5545).
generated_requirements.rationale: Calendar protocol support enables interoperability with external calendar clients such as Outlook, Google Calendar, and Apple Calendar.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with standard relational database management systems (e.g., Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, PostgreSQL) via a configurable JDBC data source.
generated_requirements.rationale: DBMS-agnostic integration via JDBC allows enterprise customers to use their existing database infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support full multilingual operation, including UI labels, notifications, and date/time formatting, with an initial minimum of English, French, German, and Spanish locales.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multilingual support is required for global enterprise deployments where employees operate in different languages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to configure organization-specific branding, including logo, color scheme, and application name, without modifying application source code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Branding configurability is required so the system can be white-labeled for each customer organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support recurring meeting reservations with configurable recurrence patterns (daily, weekly, monthly, custom), including the ability to edit a single occurrence or all future occurrences.
generated_requirements.rationale: Recurring meetings are a common enterprise scheduling pattern and must be managed with granular edit control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a personal dashboard for each user displaying their upcoming meetings, pending invitations, and any action items requiring response.
generated_requirements.rationale: A personalized dashboard improves usability by surfacing the most relevant information immediately upon login.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate users against the organization's existing identity provider, supporting LDAP/Active Directory integration and, optionally, SAML 2.0-based single sign-on.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enterprise identity integration eliminates separate credential management and aligns with corporate security policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based authorization on all API endpoints and UI actions, ensuring users cannot access or modify resources beyond their assigned role permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authorization enforcement at every layer prevents privilege escalation and unauthorized data access.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the browser client and the server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Formula Administrators to create, read, update, and delete product formulas, where each formula specifies a finished product, a list of required ingredients with quantities and units of measure, and an ordered list of substitutionary ingredients per ingredient line.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core formula management is the foundation for all downstream inventory calculations and POS integration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support semi-products within product formulas, allowing a formula ingredient line to reference another formula (semi-product) rather than a raw ingredient, and shall recursively resolve semi-product formulas when calculating total raw-ingredient consumption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Semi-product support enables multi-level bill-of-materials structures common in restaurant production workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow each ingredient line in a product formula to define an ordered substitution list, where each substitute specifies an alternative ingredient and its equivalent quantity ratio relative to the primary ingredient.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ordered substitution lists are required so the system can deterministically select alternatives when primary ingredients are out of stock.

generated_requirements.text: When generating an Inventory Quantity Adjustment (IQA) document, the system shall automatically select substitutionary ingredients in the priority order defined in the formula, using a substitute only when the current inventory level of the primary ingredient (or higher-priority substitute) is insufficient to cover the required quantity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deterministic substitution logic ensures consistent, auditable inventory deductions aligned with actual stock availability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically generate an IQA document at the end of each business day by consuming daily sales transaction data from the POS terminal, applying the relevant product formulas (including substitution resolution), and computing the net quantity adjustment for each raw ingredient.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated daily IQA generation eliminates manual calculation errors and ensures inventory records remain current.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Inventory Managers to manually trigger IQA document generation for a specified date range, in addition to the automatic end-of-day generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual triggering supports ad-hoc reconciliation and correction scenarios outside the normal daily cycle.

generated_requirements.text: Each generated IQA document shall record, at minimum: document ID, generation timestamp, business date, the POS sales data snapshot used, each ingredient adjusted, the quantity deducted, the unit of measure, whether a substitution was applied (and which substitute was used), the resulting inventory balance, and the identity of the user or process that triggered generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive IQA record fields are required to satisfy audit-compliant accounting standards and support traceability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent modification or deletion of a finalized IQA document; corrections shall only be made by creating a reversing adjustment document that references the original IQA document ID.
generated_requirements.rationale: Immutable finalized documents with reversing entries are a standard audit-compliance requirement for accounting records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Inventory Managers to create, read, update, and delete inventory items, including setting initial stock quantities, units of measure, reorder thresholds, and supplier references.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full inventory item management is necessary for maintaining accurate stock master data used in IQA calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a real-time inventory balance for each ingredient, updating balances immediately upon finalization of any IQA or manual adjustment document.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time balance updates ensure substitution logic always operates against current stock levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Formula Administrators to create, read, update, and delete hierarchical product menus, where a menu consists of categories, sub-categories, and leaf-level product items, each linked to a product formula.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical menu management enables organized product presentation and accurate formula-to-product linkage for POS integration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall propagate menu changes (additions, modifications, removals of categories or products) to all linked POS terminals within 60 seconds of the change being saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely POS synchronization prevents staff from selling products with outdated pricing, availability, or formula linkages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three predefined roles: Inventory Manager, Formula Administrator, and POS Operator, where each role is granted only the permissions necessary for its defined responsibilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC limits the blast radius of credential compromise and enforces separation of duties across staff functions.

generated_requirements.text: The Inventory Manager role shall have permissions to: view and manage inventory items and balances, view product formulas (read-only), trigger and view IQA documents, and create reversing adjustment documents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Precisely scoped Inventory Manager permissions prevent unauthorized formula or menu changes while enabling full inventory oversight.

generated_requirements.text: The Formula Administrator role shall have permissions to: create, update, and delete product formulas (including semi-products and substitution lists), and create, update, and delete hierarchical menus; Formula Administrators shall not have access to finalize or delete IQA documents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restricting Formula Administrators from IQA finalization enforces separation of duties between formula authorship and accounting records.

generated_requirements.text: The POS Operator role shall have permissions to: view the active product menu, record sales transactions, and view current product availability; POS Operators shall have no access to formula definitions, inventory balances, or IQA documents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Limiting POS Operators to sales-facing functions prevents unauthorized access to sensitive inventory and financial data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a System Administrator role with the ability to create, deactivate, and assign roles to user accounts, and to configure system-wide settings, but with no access to business transaction data.
generated_requirements.rationale: A dedicated admin role with no transaction access enforces the principle of least privilege for user management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record a tamper-evident audit log entry for every create, update, delete, and state-change operation on formulas, menus, inventory items, and IQA documents, capturing: timestamp, user ID, role, action type, affected entity ID, and before/after field values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive audit logs are required for accounting compliance and forensic investigation of data discrepancies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users with a username and password before granting access, and shall enforce a configurable password policy including minimum length (≥8 characters), complexity rules, and maximum password age.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strong authentication is the first line of defense for protecting sensitive inventory and financial records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall lock a user account after a configurable number of consecutive failed login attempts (default: 5) and require a System Administrator to unlock it.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout mitigates brute-force credential attacks on the multi-user system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an idle user session after a configurable inactivity timeout (default: 15 minutes) and require re-authentication to resume.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access from unattended workstations in a shared restaurant environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent access by multiple users simultaneously, maintaining data consistency through optimistic or pessimistic locking on shared records such as inventory balances and formula definitions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrency control prevents data corruption when multiple staff members operate the system simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly conflict notification when a concurrent edit collision is detected, presenting the conflicting changes and prompting the user to resolve the conflict before saving.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparent conflict resolution prevents silent data overwrites in a multi-user environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate that a product formula does not contain circular references (e.g., semi-product A references semi-product B which references semi-product A) and shall reject any formula save that would introduce a cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Circular formula references would cause infinite recursion during IQA calculation, making validation essential for data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent deletion of an ingredient or semi-product that is currently referenced by any active product formula, displaying a list of dependent formulas to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Referential integrity constraints prevent orphaned formula lines that would cause IQA calculation failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent deletion of a product formula that is currently linked to an active menu item, displaying the affected menu items to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting formula-to-menu linkages ensures POS terminals always have a valid formula for every sellable product.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Inventory Managers to generate inventory status reports showing current stock levels, items below reorder threshold, and projected depletion dates based on average daily consumption derived from recent IQA history.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inventory status reporting enables proactive procurement decisions and reduces stockout risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to export IQA documents and audit logs in at least one structured format (CSV or PDF) for external accounting system integration and regulatory submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Export capability is required for integration with external accounting tools and compliance reporting obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run as a Windows desktop or client-server application compatible with Windows 10 and Windows 11, and shall not require hardware upgrades beyond the restaurant's existing workstation specifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Compatibility with existing Windows hardware avoids capital expenditure and ensures smooth deployment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the automatic end-of-day IQA document generation, including full substitution resolution for all products sold, within 2 minutes for a daily sales volume of up to 1,000 distinct product transactions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A 2-minute generation ceiling ensures IQA documents are available for review before the next business day begins.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any interactive user action (form load, search, save) within 3 seconds under normal operating conditions with up to 20 concurrent users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sub-3-second response times maintain staff productivity and prevent workflow bottlenecks during peak service hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall propagate POS menu updates to all connected terminals within 60 seconds of a menu change being committed, even under peak concurrent-user load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely menu synchronization is critical to prevent POS operators from selling unavailable or incorrectly priced items.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between client workstations, POS terminals, and the server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive inventory, formula, and sales data from interception on the local network.

generated_requirements.text: All passwords stored in the system database shall be hashed using a modern adaptive hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt or Argon2) with a unique per-user salt; plaintext passwords shall never be stored or logged.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure password storage prevents credential exposure in the event of a database breach.

generated_requirements.text: The audit log shall be stored in a write-once, append-only data store or equivalent mechanism that prevents modification or deletion by any application-level user, including System Administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tamper-evident audit storage is a prerequisite for audit-compliant accounting and forensic integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% during defined business hours (e.g., 06:00–24:00 local time), excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 24 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during business hours ensures POS operations and inventory management are not disrupted during service.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated database backups at least once every 24 hours and shall support point-in-time recovery to within 1 hour of any data loss event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups with granular recovery capability protect against data loss from hardware failure or corruption.

generated_requirements.text: In the event of a server crash or network interruption during IQA document generation, the system shall automatically resume or roll back the generation process upon reconnection, ensuring no partial or duplicate IQA documents are persisted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transactional integrity during failures prevents corrupted or duplicate accounting records.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall follow a consistent visual design language across all modules (inventory, formula, menu, POS, reporting), with clearly labeled actions, confirmation dialogs for destructive operations, and inline validation messages.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent UI design reduces training time and minimizes operator errors in a fast-paced restaurant environment.

generated_requirements.text: A new staff member with basic computer literacy shall be able to complete core role-specific tasks (e.g., recording a sale, adjusting inventory, defining a formula) after no more than 4 hours of role-specific training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low training overhead is essential given the typically high staff turnover in the restaurant industry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display contextual help text or tooltips for all non-obvious fields, particularly within the formula editor (e.g., substitution priority, quantity ratio) and IQA configuration screens.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contextual help reduces reliance on external documentation and supports correct data entry for complex formula rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source code and database schema shall be documented such that a developer unfamiliar with the codebase can understand the IQA generation logic and RBAC model within 2 business days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Good documentation reduces onboarding time for future developers and lowers long-term maintenance costs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected with a modular design separating the formula engine, inventory engine, menu management, POS integration, and reporting modules, so that changes to one module do not require modifications to others.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular separation reduces regression risk when business rules in one area evolve independently of others.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce referential integrity at the database level (via foreign key constraints) for all relationships between formulas, ingredients, semi-products, menu items, and IQA documents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Database-level constraints provide a safety net against application bugs that could otherwise corrupt relational data.

generated_requirements.text: All monetary values, quantities, and unit-of-measure conversions used in IQA calculations shall be stored and computed using fixed-point decimal arithmetic with a precision of at least 4 decimal places, and floating-point types shall not be used for these fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fixed-point arithmetic prevents rounding errors that could accumulate into material inventory discrepancies over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 20 concurrent user sessions without degradation of response times beyond the thresholds defined in R031, running on the existing server hardware.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrency capacity must match the realistic number of simultaneous staff users without requiring hardware investment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all IQA documents, audit logs, and inventory transaction history for a minimum of 7 years, in compliance with standard accounting record retention requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term record retention is mandated by accounting compliance standards and supports historical audits.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrative interface for configuring role permissions, session timeout values, password policy parameters, and backup schedules without requiring code changes or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Runtime configurability allows the system to adapt to evolving business policies without developer intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the Inventory Manager via an in-application alert when any ingredient's current stock level falls below its configured reorder threshold, either in real time as balances are updated or at the time of IQA finalization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive low-stock alerts enable timely procurement actions to prevent service disruptions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed access-control violations (attempts by a user to perform an action outside their role's permissions) in the audit log, including the user ID, timestamp, and the action attempted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Logging unauthorized access attempts supports detection of insider threats and misconfigured role assignments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow new customers to register an account by providing their full name, email address, password, and country of residence.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account registration is the entry point for all customer interactions on the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to select either a pay-as-you-go plan or an unlimited subscription plan during or after registration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting dual pricing models is a core business requirement that drives revenue and customer flexibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to switch between the pay-as-you-go plan and the unlimited subscription plan from their account settings at any time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Plan flexibility improves customer retention and accommodates changing viewing habits.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to browse the movie catalog by category, genre, country of origin, release year, and language.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-faceted browsing enables discovery and caters to culturally diverse audience preferences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a full-text search capability that allows customers to search the movie catalog by title, actor, director, genre, and keyword.
generated_requirements.rationale: Robust search reduces friction in content discovery for a large and growing catalog.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow pay-as-you-go customers to purchase individual movies using a credit card or a pre-paid card.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting both payment methods broadens accessibility, especially for users without credit cards in African markets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall grant a pay-as-you-go customer a 48-hour (two-day) viewing window for a purchased movie, starting from the time of first playback.
generated_requirements.rationale: The two-day viewing window is a defined business rule that governs content access rights for transactional purchases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a pay-as-you-go customer from accessing a purchased movie after their 48-hour viewing window has expired.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforcing the viewing window protects content licensing agreements and revenue integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow unlimited subscription customers to stream any available movie in the catalog without per-title charges for the duration of their active subscription.
generated_requirements.rationale: Unlimited access is the core value proposition of the subscription plan.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to activate a pre-paid card by entering a unique card code, crediting the corresponding monetary value to their account wallet.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-paid card activation supports cash-based and unbanked customer segments prevalent in African markets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that each pre-paid card code can only be activated once, and shall display an error if a previously used or invalid code is entered.
generated_requirements.rationale: Single-use enforcement prevents fraud and double-redemption of pre-paid card value.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to stream purchased or subscription-accessible movies directly in their web browser without requiring any additional software installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser-native streaming lowers the barrier to access and aligns with the web-based platform mandate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to submit a written review and a star rating (1–5) for any movie they have watched.
generated_requirements.rationale: Community reviews drive engagement and help other customers make informed viewing decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the average star rating and all approved customer reviews on each movie's detail page.
generated_requirements.rationale: Visible ratings and reviews build social proof and support content discovery.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to manage their account profile, including updating their name, email address, password, and preferred language.
generated_requirements.rationale: Self-service account management reduces support overhead and improves user autonomy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide customers with a viewing history page listing all movies they have watched, including purchase date and viewing window status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Viewing history supports account transparency and helps customers track their content consumption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to add, edit, and remove movies from the catalog, including metadata such as title, synopsis, cast, director, genre, language, release year, and poster image.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full catalog management is essential for keeping content current and accurately described.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to create, edit, and delete movie categories and assign movies to one or more categories.
generated_requirements.rationale: Category management enables structured content organisation and improves browsability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to view, edit, suspend, and delete customer accounts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Admin control over customer accounts is necessary for policy enforcement and support resolution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to generate, issue, and deactivate pre-paid card codes, specifying the monetary value and expiry date for each batch.
generated_requirements.rationale: Admin-controlled pre-paid card lifecycle management is required to support the card-based payment channel.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to review, approve, or reject customer-submitted movie reviews before they are published on the platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: Content moderation protects the platform from inappropriate or abusive user-generated content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with a sales analytics dashboard displaying total revenue, revenue by plan type, revenue by movie title, and revenue over configurable time periods.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sales analytics are critical for business decision-making and financial oversight by management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with a usage analytics dashboard displaying total streams, most-watched movies, active subscribers, and geographic distribution of viewers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Usage analytics inform content acquisition strategy and audience understanding.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Izogn Manager to export analytics reports in CSV and PDF formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exportable reports support offline analysis and sharing with stakeholders.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send a confirmation email to the customer upon successful registration, purchase, subscription activation, and plan change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transactional emails provide customers with records of key account events and build trust.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify a customer via email when their 48-hour viewing window for a purchased movie is within 6 hours of expiry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Expiry reminders improve customer experience and reduce frustration from unexpected access loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support adaptive bitrate streaming, automatically adjusting video quality based on the customer's available bandwidth.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adaptive bitrate is essential for delivering a smooth viewing experience across variable African network conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate video playback within 5 seconds of a customer pressing the play button under normal network conditions (≥ 2 Mbps).
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast playback start reduces abandonment and meets user expectations for on-demand streaming.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return search results within 2 seconds for any catalog query under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast search response is critical to a fluid content discovery experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 concurrent streaming sessions without degradation in video quality or page responsiveness.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability to high concurrency is necessary to handle peak viewing periods and platform growth.

generated_requirements.text: All payment transactions shall be processed over TLS 1.2 or higher, and credit card data shall never be stored on the platform's servers, relying instead on a PCI-DSS-compliant payment gateway.
generated_requirements.rationale: PCI-DSS compliance and encrypted transmission are mandatory to protect customer financial data.

generated_requirements.text: All customer passwords shall be stored using a strong adaptive hashing algorithm (e.g. bcrypt or Argon2) with a unique salt per user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure password storage prevents credential exposure in the event of a database breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all administrator and Izogn Manager accounts.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA for privileged accounts significantly reduces the risk of unauthorised administrative access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement Digital Rights Management (DRM) — supporting Widevine and FairPlay — to prevent unauthorised downloading, copying, or redistribution of streamed content.
generated_requirements.rationale: DRM is required to honour content licensing agreements and protect intellectual property rights.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver all web pages and API responses exclusively over HTTPS, with HTTP requests automatically redirected to HTTPS.
generated_requirements.rationale: HTTPS-only communication protects all data in transit from interception and tampering.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints to prevent brute-force attacks, locking an account for 15 minutes after 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rate limiting and account lockout mitigate credential-stuffing and brute-force attack vectors.

generated_requirements.text: The platform's user interface shall be designed with culturally resonant visual elements, imagery, and colour palettes that reflect African — and specifically Nigerian — cultural identity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cultural resonance in UI design increases emotional connection and platform loyalty among the primary target audience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support localisation for at least English, French, Yoruba, Igbo, and Hausa, allowing customers to select their preferred interface language.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-language support makes the platform accessible to Nigeria's major language groups and broader Francophone African audiences.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support display of prices in Nigerian Naira (NGN) as the default currency, with the ability to display prices in USD, GBP, and EUR for international customers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Local currency display reduces cognitive friction for Nigerian users while multi-currency support serves the global audience.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards, ensuring usability for customers with visual, auditory, or motor impairments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance broadens the potential user base and fulfils inclusive design obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum platform availability of 99.5% uptime measured on a monthly basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability ensures customers can access content reliably, protecting revenue and user trust.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated failover so that if a primary server or CDN node fails, traffic is rerouted to a healthy node within 30 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated failover minimises service disruption and supports the availability SLA.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backups of all customer data, transaction records, and catalog metadata, with backups retained for a minimum of 30 days and restorable within 4 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups with tested restore procedures protect against data loss from failures or incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on major cloud infrastructure providers (e.g. AWS, Azure, or GCP) and shall not depend on any single proprietary hosting environment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cloud-agnostic deployment prevents vendor lock-in and supports geographic expansion closer to African user bases.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a modular architecture with documented APIs, enabling independent deployment and scaling of the streaming, payment, catalog, and analytics services.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of future changes and enables independent team ownership of services.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a comprehensive audit log of all administrative actions — including catalog changes, account modifications, and pre-paid card operations — with timestamps and actor identity, retained for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs support accountability, forensic investigation, and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use a Content Delivery Network (CDN) with Points of Presence (PoPs) in West Africa to minimise latency for Nigerian and broader African users.
generated_requirements.rationale: African CDN PoPs are critical to reducing buffering and improving streaming quality for the primary target audience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully functional on the latest two major versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge browsers on both desktop and mobile operating systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cross-browser compatibility ensures the platform is accessible to the widest possible global audience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a responsive web design that renders correctly and is fully usable on screen widths from 320px (mobile) to 2560px (large desktop).
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive design is essential given the high proportion of mobile internet users in African markets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable data protection regulations, including Nigeria's NDPR and the EU GDPR for European users, providing customers with the ability to request data export or account deletion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulatory compliance protects the business from legal liability and builds customer trust in data handling practices.
