generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures broad accessibility for all league participants regardless of their preferred browser environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support two distinct user roles: Commissioner and Team Owner, each with a defined and enforced set of permissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role separation is the foundation of league governance and access control described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require all users to authenticate via a username and password before accessing any league or team data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication is necessary to enforce role-based permissions and protect league data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that Team Owner actions are restricted to their own team, and Commissioner actions are available only to the designated commissioner account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prevents unauthorized users from performing privileged governance or cross-team roster actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a Team Owner to add an available NFL player to their roster, subject to roster size limits and waiver/free-agent rules configured by the Commissioner.
generated_requirements.rationale: Player adds are a core roster-management workflow for team owners throughout the season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a Team Owner to drop a player from their roster, immediately making that player available to other teams per league rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Player drops are a fundamental roster action that affects player availability across the league.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow two Team Owners to propose, negotiate, and either accept or reject a trade of one or more players between their rosters.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trading is a key engagement mechanic and roster-management tool in fantasy football leagues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent duplicate or conflicting roster moves (e.g., two owners simultaneously adding the same free-agent player) by applying optimistic locking or an equivalent concurrency-control mechanism.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transactional integrity during peak NFL game-day windows requires conflict prevention on shared player resources.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process and confirm any roster move (add, drop, or trade acceptance) within 3 seconds under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast transaction confirmation is critical during high-stakes, time-sensitive game-day roster decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ingest up-to-date NFL player statistics from an authoritative data provider at intervals no greater than 60 seconds during live game windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Near-real-time statistics are the core data asset driving user decisions and fantasy point calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall calculate and update each rostered player's fantasy points automatically whenever new statistical data is ingested, using the scoring rules configured for the league.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accurate, up-to-date fantasy point totals are the primary metric team owners use to evaluate their rosters.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display current-week and season-to-date fantasy points for each player on a team's roster page, refreshing automatically without requiring a full page reload.
generated_requirements.rationale: Live score visibility keeps users engaged and informed during active game windows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display detailed NFL player statistics (e.g., passing yards, rushing yards, receptions, touchdowns) on a player profile page.
generated_requirements.rationale: Granular statistics allow team owners to make informed add, drop, and trade decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to configure league-wide scoring settings, including point values assigned to each statistical category.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scoring customization is a core governance privilege that differentiates league formats.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to configure roster size limits, positional slot requirements, and roster lock times.
generated_requirements.rationale: Roster rules are fundamental league settings that govern how team owners manage their teams.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to configure waiver wire rules, including waiver order, waiver processing schedule, and free-agent acquisition budgets (FAAB) if applicable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Waiver rules are a key governance setting that controls fairness in player acquisition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to configure trade review periods, trade veto rules, and the deadline after which no trades are permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trade governance settings protect league integrity and are an exclusive commissioner privilege.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to manually override or reverse a completed roster move in cases of system error or rule violation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual override capability is an elevated governance action required to correct erroneous transactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce roster lock rules by preventing any roster changes for players whose NFL game has already kicked off, as determined by the official game schedule.
generated_requirements.rationale: Roster locking is a fundamental fairness rule that prevents owners from exploiting live game information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a league message board where any league participant can create posts and reply to existing threads.
generated_requirements.rationale: A message board fosters community engagement among league participants throughout the season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall surface the most recent message board posts (minimum 5, configurable) on the league home page to promote community visibility.
generated_requirements.rationale: Surfacing recent posts on the league page drives engagement without requiring users to navigate to the full board.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to moderate the message board by deleting posts or threads that violate league conduct rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Moderation is a governance privilege necessary to maintain a respectful league community.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide intuitive navigation allowing users to move between the league home page and any team page within two clicks or fewer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Minimal navigation depth reduces friction and supports the fast, fluid experience expected by engaged fantasy players.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render any league or team page with a Time to First Contentful Paint (FCP) of under 2 seconds on a standard broadband connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast page loads are essential for user satisfaction, especially during high-traffic game-day periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an uptime of 99.9% or greater during the NFL regular season and playoff windows (September through February).
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is critical during the seasonal usage window when all user transactions occur.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 concurrent users without degradation in response time beyond defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Peak NFL game-day windows drive high concurrency that the platform must sustain without performance loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally by adding compute capacity automatically in response to traffic spikes exceeding 80% CPU or memory utilization thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auto-scaling ensures the platform remains responsive during unpredictable NFL game-day traffic surges.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store all user passwords using a strong, salted hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt or Argon2) and shall never store or transmit passwords in plaintext.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure password storage protects user credentials in the event of a data breach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall transmit all data between client and server exclusively over HTTPS using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encrypted transport prevents interception of sensitive user and league data in transit.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall invalidate user sessions after 30 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication to resume.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout limits the window of exposure from unattended or hijacked sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit log of all roster moves, trade actions, and commissioner configuration changes, including the acting user, timestamp, and before/after state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs are essential for dispute resolution and governance accountability in a competitive league environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall guarantee that each roster transaction (add, drop, trade) is atomic — either fully committed or fully rolled back — with no partial state persisted to the database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Atomic transactions prevent data corruption and inconsistent roster states during concurrent peak-usage periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly error message when a roster move is rejected due to a rule violation (e.g., roster full, player locked, trade deadline passed), specifying the reason for rejection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Descriptive error messages reduce user frustration and support self-service resolution without commissioner intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send an email or in-app notification to all relevant parties when a trade is proposed, accepted, rejected, or vetoed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications keep trade participants informed and reduce the risk of missed deadlines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send an email or in-app notification to a Team Owner when a waiver claim they submitted is processed, indicating success or failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Waiver result notifications are critical for owners to react quickly and adjust their roster strategy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a searchable and filterable player pool view, allowing Team Owners to search by player name, NFL team, position, and availability status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Efficient player discovery is essential for owners evaluating add and trade targets across a large player pool.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display each team's current standings, win-loss record, and total points scored on the league home page.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standings are a primary engagement driver and a key reference point for all league participants.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current week's matchup for each team, including live scores for both teams, on the team page.
generated_requirements.rationale: Live matchup scores are the most-viewed feature during game windows and central to the platform's value.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to create a new league by specifying league name, number of teams, draft date, and initial rule settings.
generated_requirements.rationale: League creation is the entry-point workflow that initializes all subsequent league activity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the Commissioner to invite Team Owners to join the league via a unique invitation link or email, and track invitation acceptance status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Invitation management is necessary to populate the league with the correct set of participants before the season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform automated daily backups of all league, roster, and transaction data, with a recovery point objective (RPO) of no more than 24 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups protect against data loss from system failures during the critical NFL season window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned outage and restore full service within a recovery time objective (RTO) of 1 hour.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined RTO ensures the platform can resume operations quickly during the time-sensitive NFL season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular, service-oriented architecture to allow individual components (e.g., statistics ingestion, scoring engine, message board) to be updated or replaced independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the risk and effort of future enhancements and bug fixes during the live season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a responsive UI that adapts to mobile, tablet, and desktop screen sizes, ensuring all core workflows are fully functional on each form factor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mobile responsiveness is essential as many users manage their teams from smartphones during live games.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall rate-limit API endpoints to prevent abuse, allowing no more than 100 requests per minute per authenticated user session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rate limiting protects the platform from automated abuse and denial-of-service attempts during peak periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all user inputs on both the client and server side to prevent injection attacks (SQL injection, XSS) and enforce data integrity constraints.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dual-layer input validation is a baseline security control against common web application attack vectors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrative dashboard for the Commissioner showing league health metrics, including pending trades, waiver claims, and unread message board posts.
generated_requirements.rationale: A consolidated dashboard reduces the commissioner's effort to monitor and govern all active league activity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed authentication attempts and automatically lock an account for 15 minutes after 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Account lockout policies mitigate brute-force credential attacks against user accounts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose all internal data integrations (e.g., NFL statistics provider) through versioned API contracts to ensure stability when the upstream provider updates its schema.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned API contracts isolate the platform from breaking changes in third-party data sources during the live season.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a projected fantasy point total for each rostered player for the current week, based on historical performance and upcoming matchup data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Projected points are a high-value decision-support feature that helps owners optimize their starting lineups.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a four-level organizational hierarchy consisting of branch, complex, district, and regional levels, and shall enforce this hierarchy in all budget submission, data visibility, and approval workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: The multi-level PCG hierarchy is the core structural model driving all budget workflows and access rules in the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized branch-level users to create, edit, and submit annual budget entries for their assigned branch.
generated_requirements.rationale: Branch-level staff are the primary data entry actors in the budgeting cycle and must be able to initiate submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow complex-level managers to review, consolidate, and approve or reject budget submissions from all branches within their complex.
generated_requirements.rationale: Complex managers are responsible for aggregating branch submissions before escalation to district level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow district-level managers to review, consolidate, and approve or reject budget submissions from all complexes within their district.
generated_requirements.rationale: District managers perform the second tier of review and consolidation in the approval chain.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow regional-level managers to review, consolidate, and finalize budget submissions from all districts within their region, producing the final approved budget.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regional managers represent the top of the approval hierarchy and produce the authoritative budget output.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict each user's data visibility strictly to the organizational unit(s) they are authorized for, preventing cross-branch, cross-complex, or cross-district data access unless the user's role explicitly permits it.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data isolation by organizational unit is essential to maintain confidentiality and integrity of budget submissions across the hierarchy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the User Administration System (UAS) to authenticate all users and retrieve their assigned roles, organizational unit assignments, and access permissions prior to granting access to any system functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralized authentication and authorization via UAS ensures consistent, single-source-of-truth access control across the application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize user role and permission data from the UAS at least once every 24 hours, and shall immediately reflect any real-time role changes pushed by the UAS within 15 minutes of the change being made.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely synchronization ensures that role changes (e.g., staff reassignments during the budget cycle) are reflected promptly without requiring a full re-login.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to all functionality and display an appropriate error message if the UAS is unavailable at the time of login, and shall not permit access based on cached credentials alone.
generated_requirements.rationale: Preventing access when UAS is unavailable ensures that stale or revoked permissions cannot be exploited.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load revenue and expense data for each organizational unit directly from the Business Data Warehouse (BDW) at the start of each budgeting cycle and on a scheduled refresh basis throughout the cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BDW is the authoritative source of financial data and must seed the budgeting system to ensure accuracy of baseline figures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the timestamp of the most recent successful BDW data load to users on all budget entry screens, so they are aware of the currency of the underlying data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparency about data freshness helps users make informed decisions and reduces errors caused by stale data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and handle BDW data load failures gracefully, logging the failure with full error details, alerting the system administrator, and preventing users from submitting budgets based on incomplete or stale data until the issue is resolved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Failure handling for BDW integration is critical to prevent corrupt or incomplete budget submissions during the high-stakes budgeting period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply the current year's approved budget formulae automatically to BDW-loaded revenue and expense data to calculate projected figures for each organizational unit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula-driven calculations are the core mechanism by which raw financial data is transformed into budget projections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated formula management module accessible only to authorized finance administrators, allowing them to define, update, and version-control the budget calculation formulae prior to the start of each annual budgeting cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Controlled formula management ensures that only authorized personnel can alter the calculations that drive all budget projections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a full version history of all budget formulae, recording the formula definition, the identity of the user who created or modified it, and the date and time of each change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Version history supports auditability and enables rollback if an erroneous formula is published.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that updated formulae pass a validation step — including syntax checking and test execution against a sample dataset — before they can be published as the active formula set for the budgeting cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Validation prevents erroneous formulae from propagating to all users during the time-sensitive budgeting period.

generated_requirements.text: Upon publication of a new formula set, the system shall automatically recalculate all in-progress budget projections for all organizational units using the updated formulae and notify affected users of the recalculation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic propagation ensures all users are working with consistent, up-to-date projections after a formula update.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an audit log that records all user actions including logins, data edits, budget submissions, approvals, rejections, and formula changes, capturing the user identity, timestamp, action type, and affected data entity.
generated_requirements.rationale: A comprehensive audit log is essential for accountability, compliance, and post-hoc investigation of budget discrepancies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to submit budget entries with optional free-text comments or justifications attached to individual line items or to the overall submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Narrative comments provide context for reviewers at higher hierarchy levels and support informed approval decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify users via in-system alerts and email when a budget submission assigned to them requires action (e.g., has been returned for revision, approved, or is approaching a deadline).
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive notifications reduce delays in the approval chain and keep all stakeholders informed of submission status.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard for each user role that displays the current status of all budget submissions within their scope, including counts of submitted, pending, approved, and rejected items.
generated_requirements.rationale: A role-scoped dashboard gives managers at every level immediate visibility into the progress of the budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers at complex, district, and regional levels to generate consolidated budget summary reports aggregating data from all subordinate organizational units within their scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consolidated reporting is a core output of the budgeting process and is required for management decision-making.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to export budget data and reports in at least CSV and PDF formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Export capability supports downstream use of budget data in other tools and satisfies reporting obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable annual budgeting calendar, including submission deadlines at each hierarchy level, and shall prevent submissions or approvals outside the defined active budgeting window unless an administrator grants an extension.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deadline enforcement ensures the budgeting cycle completes on schedule and prevents late or unauthorized modifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a finance administrator to lock a submitted and approved budget at any hierarchy level to prevent further modification, and shall record the lock action in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Budget locking protects finalized figures from accidental or unauthorized changes after approval.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that each user can only access the screens, actions, and data records that correspond to their assigned role and organizational unit, with no privilege escalation possible through the UI or API.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict RBAC is the primary security control preventing unauthorized access to sensitive financial data across the hierarchy.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the user's browser and the RFS server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive financial data from interception on the intranet.

generated_requirements.text: All data stored in the RFS database, including budget figures, audit logs, and formula definitions, shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent approved algorithm.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption at rest protects sensitive financial data in the event of unauthorized physical or logical database access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate an authenticated user session after 30 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication via the UAS before access is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access from unattended workstations in a shared office environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% uptime during the defined annual budgeting period, measured on a monthly basis, excluding pre-approved maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during the budgeting period is critical because downtime directly blocks time-sensitive financial submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.0% uptime during non-budgeting periods, excluding pre-approved maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Baseline availability outside the peak period ensures the system remains accessible for reporting and administrative tasks year-round.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any user-initiated page load or data retrieval request within 3 seconds under normal load conditions (up to 200 concurrent users) during the budgeting period.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive page loads are essential to user productivity during the high-pressure, time-constrained budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete a full BDW data load for all organizational units within 60 minutes of the scheduled load initiation time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely data loads ensure that users have access to current financial data without extended waiting periods.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the recalculation of all in-progress budget projections following a formula update within 30 minutes for the full organizational dataset.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fast formula propagation minimizes disruption to users working on budget submissions when formulae are updated.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support at least 300 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of response times beyond the thresholds defined in R032.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system must handle peak concurrent usage across all hierarchy levels during the budgeting period without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to ENET Securities' intranet UI standards for layout, typography, color palette, navigation patterns, and branding, as defined in the current ENET Intranet Style Guide.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conformance to intranet standards ensures a consistent user experience and satisfies organizational governance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully functional on the browser versions specified in ENET Securities' intranet browser compatibility matrix, including the current and immediately preceding major versions of approved browsers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser compatibility ensures all finance staff can access the system regardless of which approved browser is installed on their workstation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help text and tooltips on all budget entry fields, formula-driven calculated fields, and submission workflow actions to guide users unfamiliar with the budgeting process.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline guidance reduces training overhead and user errors during the annual budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display clear, user-friendly validation error messages on all data entry forms, identifying the specific field(s) in error and providing guidance on acceptable values, before allowing a submission to proceed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Descriptive validation feedback prevents incomplete or invalid budget submissions and reduces back-and-forth between users and reviewers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain data integrity by enforcing referential consistency between organizational hierarchy records, budget line items, and BDW-sourced data, rejecting any transaction that would create orphaned or inconsistent records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Referential integrity ensures the financial data model remains consistent and trustworthy throughout the budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated daily backups of all budget data, formula definitions, audit logs, and configuration data, with backups retained for a minimum of 90 days and restorable within 4 hours of a declared incident.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regular backups with defined recovery objectives protect against data loss during the critical budgeting period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular, layered architecture that separates presentation, business logic, and data access concerns, enabling individual components to be updated or replaced without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture reduces the cost and risk of annual formula updates, integration changes, and UI standard revisions.

generated_requirements.text: All integration interfaces with the BDW and UAS shall be implemented using documented, versioned APIs, and the system shall log all integration calls including request parameters, response codes, and latency for diagnostic purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Versioned, logged integration interfaces simplify troubleshooting of data load failures and access control issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a health-monitoring dashboard for system administrators displaying real-time status of BDW integration, UAS integration, scheduled job execution, and system resource utilization.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational visibility enables administrators to detect and respond to integration or performance issues before they impact users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent concurrent editing of the same budget record by multiple users by implementing an optimistic locking mechanism, notifying a user if their save attempt conflicts with a concurrent change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrency control prevents data loss or corruption when multiple users attempt to edit the same budget record simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all failed login attempts and alert the system administrator if more than five consecutive failed login attempts are detected for a single user account within a 10-minute window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Brute-force detection protects user accounts from unauthorized access attempts on the intranet.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a configurable scheduled maintenance window during which the system may be taken offline, and shall display a pre-configured maintenance notice to users at least 24 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Advance maintenance notices allow finance staff to plan their work around system downtime, especially during the budgeting period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a finance administrator to configure and manage the organizational hierarchy (adding, modifying, or deactivating branches, complexes, districts, and regions) without requiring a code change or system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configuration-driven hierarchy management accommodates organizational restructuring without incurring development effort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all historical budget submissions, approvals, audit logs, and formula versions for a minimum of seven years to support regulatory and internal audit requirements.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term data retention satisfies financial regulatory obligations and supports multi-year trend analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data received from the BDW during a load operation against expected schema, data types, and value ranges, rejecting and flagging any records that fail validation rather than silently loading corrupt or malformed data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation on BDW data prevents malformed financial figures from propagating into budget calculations and submissions.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be available 24/7 especially during the budgeting period. The RFS system shall be available 90% of the time all year and 98% during the budgeting period. 2% of the time the system will become available within 1 hour of the time that the situation is reported.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum hardware requirements prior to implementation. Minimum Hard Disk Space: 400MB.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum software requirements prior to implementation: Microsoft Internet Explorer 5.0. Microsoft .NET Framework 1.1. Microsoft Internet Explorer Web Controls
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be able to easily interface with the BDW environment in order to load the data. Seven data files shall be received from the BDW and loaded into the RFS system within 3 hours one day prior to the start of budgeting process every year. The data will be as of October month-end. Test files (7) shall be loaded up to 5 times throughout the year from the BDW.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Changes to revenue and expense formulae must be applied to the RFS system every year prior to the budgeting process start. Revenue and expense formulae will be updated within one week of their receipt after which 2 weeks will be allotted to testing of the budget calculations to be conducted jointly by the project development team and PCG Finance.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: New branches/complexes/districts/regions and revenue/expense accounts added to the BDW must be reflected in the RFS system. 100% of the entities (mentioned above) available as of October month-end will be loaded into the RFS system prior to the budget start process.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only authorized user shall access the RFS system. ONLY users that exist in the UAS system with the RFS system assigned to their role(s) shall access the RFS system and use it.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: User access should be limited to the permissions granted to their role(s) Each level in the PCG hierarchy will be assigned a role and users will be assigned to these roles. Access to functionality within RFS system is dependent on the privileges/permission assigned to the role.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product must comply with the intranet page standards and requirements of ENET Securities. 95% of the product look & feel will be approved by the Architecture group prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected and approved within one month of product release.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The intranet pages should display appropriately in all resolutions from 800x600 and higher. The display of all the pages will be correct in 95% of the resolutions prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected within a week of notification of the incorrect display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The users should be able to easily use the system to successfully complete their budgets within the stipulated time for completion. With a week of training prior to product implementation 98% of the users shall complete their budgets within the stipulated time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product interface should be fast. The response time of the product interface should not exceed 2 seconds 90% of the time. For 10% of the time the response time should not be more than 10 seconds.not be more than 10 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR
